summary: Propagate lock-read errors in the two remaining swallows (guard's mode_from_lock, coverage_note's with_locked_kinds); reject an out-of-vocabulary enforcement-mode value loud

edit.description: `mode_from_lock` (line 500) `.unwrap_or_default()`s the whole `read_declarations` read — an unreadable, unparseable, or row-malformed lock silently reads as absent, running the guard at default `warn` even where the lock declared `block` — and degrades an out-of-vocabulary `mode` value to the default. Make it fallible: propagate the read error (the Guard arm already returns miette::Result) and reject an unknown `mode` value loud. A genuinely missing lock is already Ok(default) inside read_declarations (drift.rs:2235), so legitimate absence keeps the documented default-warn posture.

`with_locked_kinds` (line 217) `.unwrap_or_default()`s the same read: a corrupt lock reads as "no kinds declared", silently dropping the locked-kind suppression, while the row-lift half (`from_kind_fact_row`) is already loud. Propagate the read error through the function's existing `Result` return; keep the missing-lock degrade-to-`kinds`-alone the doc comment names.

tests.asserts: `temper guard` over a workspace whose lock.toml is corrupt (invalid TOML, or a malformed/out-of-vocabulary `mode` assembly row) errors loud, never silently degrading a declared `block` to `warn`; a missing lock still guards at default `warn`
the coverage note over a corrupt lock rejects loud instead of silently degrading to built-ins-only suppression; a missing lock still degrades to the built-in kinds alone

acceptance: No caller of drift::read_declarations discards its error: a corrupt lock is an error at every placement (intent invariant 6; distribution's fail-loud invariant) and an out-of-vocabulary enforcement-mode value is rejected, never defaulted — while a genuinely absent lock keeps its documented default posture everywhere.
notes: Residue of b3ffb52 (LOCK-ROW-REJECT-LOUD): the mode_from_lock half is that commit's own declared queue residual; the with_locked_kinds read-swallow was found this sweep — the commit body claimed coverage_note propagates, but only the row lift is loud, not the read at :217. rg confirms these are the only two swallowing callers of read_declarations. Scoped at 1817f39.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 3024687 (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
